vulnerability.reason: Both reference and generated describe sensitivity to overfitting due to lack of out-of-distribution robustness checks, with generated framing it via engineers' reliance but same condition.
vulnerability.reference: LLM-driven search overfits to training distribution without out-of-distribution robustness checks.
vulnerability.generated: Engineers rely on LLM-discovered structures without out-of-distribution validation, leaving deployed classifiers unable to resist accuracy loss from overfitting.

impact.reason: Generated captures misclassification leading to erroneous decisions, but omits the safety-critical task context, missing the affected party's criticality.
impact.reference: Misclassification of out-of-distribution images leads to incorrect decisions in safety-critical tasks.
impact.generated: Misclassification of out-of-distribution images degrades classifier reliability, leading to erroneous automated decisions in deployed vision systems.

dose_response.reason: Same causal arc: overfitting search yields brittle structures, deployed without OOD validation, leading to misclassification; extra intermediate steps do not alter the core mechanism.
dose_response.reference: Overfitting LLM-driven search yields brittle tensor networks that, when deployed by computer vision engineers without out-of-distribution robustness checks, cause misclassification in safety-critical tasks.
dose_response.generated: Automated LLM-driven algorithm generation overfits to optimization criteria, producing brittle structures that fail under distribution shifts, through computer vision engineers deploying classifiers without out-of-distribution validation, triggers reliance on unvalidated structures that lose accuracy, leading to misclassification and erroneous automated decisions.
